skill.name: ChatCrystal Task Writeback
skill.description: Write reusable ChatCrystal task memories after substantive work completes. Use when implementation or debugging produced a durable fix, pitfall, pattern, or decision worth preserving, and when the environment can either persist it through write_task_memory or emit a structured memory candidate for later save.
skill.summary: Automatically capture and preserve reusable knowledge from completed development tasks into ChatCrystal memory.
skill.author: ZengLiangYi

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 32 potential security issues, all of which are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' warnings are caused by Markdown code syntax (inline code with backticks) in the SKILL.md documentation file, not actual shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' warnings are scanner errors with no cryptographic code present. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, network operations, or command execution capabilities. Safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Lose valuable insights from debugging and implementation sessions. This skill automatically preserves reusable fixes, patterns, and decisions into ChatCrystal memory for future reference.
actual_capabilities: Automatically detect when completed work produces reusable knowledge
Structure memory entries with outcome type, root cause, and resolution
Integrate with ChatCrystal MCP write_task_memory tool when available
Degrade gracefully to emit memory candidates when persistence is unavailable
Support project-scoped and global knowledge scopes
Handle memory skip, merge, and create decisions from Core
limitations: Requires ChatCrystal Core and MCP access for automatic persistence
Only captures knowledge when explicitly invoked after task completion
Cannot retroactively capture knowledge from past sessions
Requires stable run or session key for automatic writeback mode

use_cases.title: Debugging Session Capture
use_cases.description: After fixing a complex bug, automatically preserve the root cause analysis, error signatures, and resolution for future reference.

use_cases.title: Pattern Documentation
use_cases.description: When implementing a reusable architectural pattern, capture the design decision and context for team-wide reuse.

use_cases.title: Knowledge Base Building
use_cases.description: Continuously build a knowledge base of fixes and pitfalls encountered during development for faster future troubleshooting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Memory Capture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback to save the outcome of this task.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple invocation after completing any task with reusable insights

prompt_templates.title: Debugging Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback to persist this bug fix. Include error signatures, root cause, and the files that were modified.
prompt_templates.scenario: After debugging and fixing an issue with specific error patterns

prompt_templates.title: Pattern Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback to document this architectural pattern as a decision record with the implementation context.
prompt_templates.scenario: When implementing a design pattern that should be reused across the project

prompt_templates.title: Manual Save Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback in manual mode to save this knowledge globally with explicit confirmation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Explicitly saving cross-project knowledge via manual save workflow

output_examples.input: Use chatcrystal-task-writeback to save this fix
output_examples.output: Memory created: MCP tool schemas must stay aligned with memory service request shapes; mismatches surface as validation failures before tool execution.
Memory merged: Added new error signature 'invalid_type' to existing memory about schema validation issues.
Memory skipped: Similar pattern already exists in project memory. Task result preserved without duplication.

best_practices: Invoke the skill only after substantive work completes, not during active problem-solving
Write concise, durable summaries that remain valuable weeks later
Include error signatures and file paths for debugging-related memories
Use project scope by default, global scope only for cross-project knowledge
Report persistence results accurately (created, merged, skipped) to track knowledge capture
anti_patterns: Calling the skill during active debugging instead of after resolution
Writing chatty or temporal summaries that lose value quickly
Using manual mode as a silent fallback instead of explicit user-initiated saves
Forcing persistence when Core reports the memory should be skipped
Instructing runtimes to auto-install ChatCrystal Core as part of skill execution

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use this skill after completing substantive work that produced reusable knowledge: a durable fix, a meaningful pitfall, a reusable pattern, or an explicit engineering decision.

faq.question: What happens if ChatCrystal is not available?
faq.answer: The skill degrades gracefully and emits a structured memory candidate aligned with the write_task_memory payload shape for manual saving later.

faq.question: What is the difference between auto and manual mode?
faq.answer: Auto mode is for automatic writeback with a stable run key. Manual mode is for explicit user-initiated save flows, never as a silent fallback.

faq.question: Should I use project or global scope?
faq.answer: Default to project scope for project-specific knowledge. Use global scope only for clearly cross-project knowledge and only in manual mode.

faq.question: What does it mean when memory is skipped or merged?
faq.answer: ChatCrystal Core analyzes the memory for duplicates and related memories. Skipped means a duplicate exists. Merged means the memory was combined with related knowledge.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without MCP access?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill will emit a structured memory candidate that you can save manually later, but automatic persistence requires MCP access to write_task_memory.
